channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Person accused of accosting Rep. Nancy Mace at Capitol pleads not guilty to assault charge
item.description: A person accused of accosting U.S. Rep. Nancy Mace in a Capitol Office building has pleaded not guilty to a misdemeanor assault charge.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Garland lauds 'principled' Wray for 'integrity and skill' after resignation announcement
item.description: AG Garland praises FBI Director Wray's multi-decade career as a civil servant and U.S. prosecutor, including U.S. assistant AG and head of the Justice Department's Criminal Division.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hegseth meets with moderate Sen. Collins as he lobbies for key votes in the Senate
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump’s nominee to lead the Defense Department said he had a “wonderful conversation” with Maine Sen.
item.dc:creator: Mary Clare Jalonick And Matt Brown, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: House small business panel releases year-end report on 'partisan' Biden agency electioneering allegations
item.description: The House Small Business Committee released its long-awaited report on the SBA's memo of understanding with the Michigan Dept of State over voter registration and alleged partisan electioneering.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republicans looking for new ways to force through China crackdowns left out of yearly defense bill
item.description: After a number of China crackdown provisions failed to make it into the yearly defense bill, Republicans are working on ways to get them signed into law before the end of the year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Democratic senators urge Biden to act on temporary protections for migrants
item.description: Democratic senators are urging President Joe Biden to extend temporary protections for migrants in the U.S. before he leaves office.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Groves, Colleen Long And Gisela Salomon, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Mast blasts Blinken over 'tens of billions' of US taxpayer dollars sent to Taliban post-Afghanistan withdrawal
item.description: Secretary of State Antony Blinken was grilled by the incoming chair of the House Foreign Relations Committee on "billions" of U.S. dollars sent to Taliban.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: FBI Director Wray says he intends to resign before Trump takes office in January
item.description: FBI Director Christopher Wray says he plans to resign at the end of President Joe Biden’s term in January.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: FBI Director Christopher Wray announces resignation
item.description: FBI Director Christopher Wray announced he is retiring from his post in January at the end of the current administration and, until then, will focus on the agency's mission.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump trolls Trudeau as 'governor' of Canada
item.description: "It was a pleasure to have dinner the other night with Governor Justin Trudeau of the Great State of Canada," Trump wrote in a post on Truth Social.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Rising US-Mexico tension a game of optics, analyst says
item.description: Change is coming to the border on Jan. 20, when President-elect Trump takes office. How profound and lasting such change might be remains to be seen, but one thing is certain: Optics will play a big role in Washington, D.C., and in Mexico City.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, NewsNation

item.title: Cornyn warns Hegseth faces 'unpleasant' confirmation hearing
item.description: Sen. John Cornyn (R-Texas) says Pete Hegseth, President-elect Trump’s pick to head the Department of Defense, faces a nasty Senate confirmation hearing that would likely be uglier than the battle over Supreme Court Justice Brett Kavanaugh in 2018.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Jeffries: Democrats not telling members how to vote on NDAA
item.description: House Democratic leaders will not tell their members how to vote on the measure to reauthorize Pentagon spending that is hitting the floor Wednesday afternoon. House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: GOP senators say they won’t fight Trump on Jan. 6 pardons
item.description: Republican senators who witnessed firsthand the Jan. 6, 2021, ransacking of congressional offices now say it’s up to President-elect Donald Trump whether to pardon those protesters convicted of federal crimes.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's picks for key positions in his second administration
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has filled the key posts for his second term in office, prioritizing loyalty to him after he felt bruised and hampered by internal squabbling during his first term.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Donald Trump is returning to the world stage. So is his trolling
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump’s recent dinner with Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau and his visit to Paris for the reopening of the Notre Dame Cathedral were not just exercises in policy and diplomacy. They were also prime trolling opportunities for Trump.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE and ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Report on attempts to kill Trump urges Secret Service to limit protection of foreign leaders
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A congressional task force investigating the attempts to kill Donald Trump during his presidential campaign is recommending changes to the Secret Service, including protecting fewer foreign leaders during the height of election season and considering moving the agency out of the Ho
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House advances annual defense policy bill, teeing up final vote
item.description: The House advanced the annual defense policy bill on Tuesday, teeing up the mammoth measure for a final vote later this week. The chamber voted 211-207 along party lines to adopt the rule — which governs debate for legislation — for the sprawling bill, formally opening up debate in the chamber.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Biden stakes out economic legacy at Brookings as Trump readies takeover
item.description: President Biden on Tuesday made the case for the strength of his economic legacy as he prepared to leave office next month, arguing President-elect Trump would be inheriting a desirable situation and warning of the risks of the incoming administration’s proposals.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: New Yorkers could receive 'inflation refund' check from state: Here's how much
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Millions of New Yorkers would receive hundreds of dollars in an “inflation refund” payment from the state next year under a new proposal from Gov. Kathy Hochul.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11

item.title: House panel holds drone hearing
item.description: Capitol Hill has taken notice of recent, mysterious drone sightings across the Northeast and is hoping to get to the bottom of the mystery.
item.dc:creator: Joe Khalil, NewsNation

item.title: Biden to tout economic legacy in Tuesday speech
item.description: President Biden will deliver remarks Tuesday afternoon touting his economic agenda and making the case that it has been more effective than the kinds of tax cuts likely to be implemented by the incoming Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Nevada adds blockchain security to prevent repeat of 'fake electors' mischief
item.description: Nevada has integrated blockchain technology into its election certification process to add even more security to its procedures, the Nevada Secretary of State's Office announced on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US hostage envoy in Beirut to seek information on missing journalist Austin Tice
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. government's top hostage negotiator is in Beirut in hopes of collecting information on the whereabouts of Austin Tice, an American journalist missing in Syria for 12 years, the State Department said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Social Security beneficiaries at risk of delays amid GOP funding fight
item.description: Social Security beneficiaries could face longer wait times for service next year unless Congress agrees to the White House budget office’s request to increase funding for the Social Security Administration (SSA), the administration that runs the program is warning.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Mayoral hopeful Michael Blake discusses vision for NYC
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – As we approach 2025, the new year marks a new election cycle. Political consultant Michael Blake joins PIX on Politics to discuss his decision to join the long list of Democrats competing with Mayor Eric Adams in next year’s mayoral primary.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Congressman Torres blasts crime in NYC
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The cold-blooded shooting of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson in the heart of Manhattan shocked New Yorkers, including Congressman Ritchie Torres, leaving the lawmaker to pose the question: “What is happening in New York City?” Torres joined PIX on Politics to discuss city crim
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Ocasio-Cortez seeks to lead Democrats on Oversight panel
item.description: Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (D-N.Y.) on Friday formally launched a bid to lead Democrats on the Oversight and Accountability Committee in the next Congress — a powerful seat that will play a prominent role in the party's effort to push back against President Trump in his second term.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, Fox 5

item.title: Trump nominates Rodney Scott to lead Customs and Border Protection
item.description: Rodney Scott at CBP and Caleb Vitello at ICE would work alongside Stephen Miller, who was named deputy chief of staff for policy and Tom Homan, also tapped to be a "border czar.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Martínez-Beltrán, MPR News

item.title: Minnesota U.S. Attorney Andy Luger to step down ahead of Trump transition
item.description: U.S. Attorney Andy Luger is stepping down soon as the top federal law enforcement official in Minnesota. As is custom for political appointees, he’s resigning to make way for a yet-to-be-announced successor chosen by incoming President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Matt Sepic, MPR News

item.title: Elon Musk and Vivek Ramaswamy are bringing Trump’s DOGE to Capitol Hill
item.description: The leaders of the so-called “Department of Government Efficiency” are calling for large-scale layoffs of federal workers and the elimination of some federal agencies during Trump's second term.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News
